channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Leader of Georgia's separatist region of Abkhazia resigns, ending unrest
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The separatist leader of Georgia's breakaway region of Abkhazia resigned on Tuesday after days of unrest, in which the opposition and its supporters seized key government buildings and at least 14 people were injured in clashes with police.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: School closures and travel delays as Arctic air brings snow and sleet to parts of the UK
item.description: Dozens of schools were closed and road and train travel ground to a halt as a cold snap brought snow, ice and sleet across parts of the UK.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia says Ukraine fired 6 US-made ATACMS at the Bryansk region, days after Biden policy shift
item.description: The Russian Defense Ministry says that Ukraine fired six U.S.-made ATACMS missiles at Russia’s Bryansk region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Putin lowers the threshold for using his nuclear arsenal after Biden's arms decision for Ukraine
item.description: President Vladimir Putin formally lowered the threshold for Russia’s use of nuclear weapons, following U.S. President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russian territory with American-supplied longer-range missiles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tens of thousands crowd New Zealand's Parliament grounds in support of Māori rights
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — As tens of thousands crowded the streets in New Zealand’s capital, Wellington, on Tuesday, the throng of people, flags aloft, had the air of a festival or a parade rather than a protest.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy arrives in Lebanon to meet with officials about possible cease-fire
item.description: A U.S. envoy has arrived in Beirut to meet with Lebanese officials about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands of UK farmers descend on Parliament to protest a tax they say will ruin family farms
item.description: British farmers have descending on Parliament to protest a tax hike they say will deal a “hammer blow” to struggling family farms.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: G20 summit calls for more aid to Gaza and an end to the war in Ukraine
item.description: Leaders of the world’s 20 major economies have gathered once again with a lighter schedule after they issued a joint declaration the night before.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Eléonore Hughes And David Biller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s G20 summit produces a broad declaration that’s short on specifics
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — The summit of the Group of 20 leading economies in Rio de Janeiro produced a joint declaration Monday that, while not totally endorsed by one of the group's members, succeeded in addressing most topics host Brazil had prioritized addressing: both ongoing major wars, a global pa
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, MAURICIO SAVARESE and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden’s Ukraine move tests Putin, riles Trump allies
item.description: The Biden administration is apparently calling Russian leader Vladimir Putin’s bluff by allowing Ukraine to strike far into Russian territory with U.S. weapons systems, a move that has drawn the ire of President-elect Trump’s allies.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Cruz says UN draft resolution against Israel is aimed at undermining incoming Trump admin, GOP Congress
item.description: In the closing weeks of the Biden administration, there are new concerns in Israel and among supporters of the Jewish state that the Biden administration will seek to punish Israel at the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Top House Democrat: Gabbard 'a national security threat' to US
item.description: Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-N.Y.), the ranking member on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Monday that former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), now a Republican and President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, is “a national security threat” to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Russia: US adding 'fuel to the fire' with Ukraine missiles decision
item.description: The Kremlin on Monday issued an angry response to a reported U.S. decision to allow Ukraine to use Western-provided missiles to strike deep into Russian territory, accusing Washington of adding “fuel to the fire” in a war Moscow launched almost three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Negotiators are urged to get down to business as climate talks in Baku enter second week
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — United Nations climate talks resumed Monday with negotiators urged to make progress on a stalled-out deal that could see developing countries get more money to spend on clean energy and adapting to climate-charged weather extremes. U.N.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, MELINA WALLING and SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Typhoon Man-yi leaves 7 dead in Philippines and worsens crisis from back-to-back storms
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Typhoon Man-yi left at least seven people dead in a landslide, destroyed houses and displaced large numbers of villagers before blowing away from the northern Philippines, worsening the crisis wreaked by multiple back-to-back storms, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US and Philippines sign a pact to secure shared military intelligence and weapons technology
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The United States and the Philippines signed an agreement on Monday to secure the exchange of highly confidential military intelligence and technology in key weapons the U.S. would provide to Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump compiles his pro-Israel team: Here are the key players
item.description: President-elect Trump is compiling one of the most pro-Israel teams of any administration in history, following a presidential battle that saw Vice President Harris lose support from voters upset the Biden administration armed Israel in its war against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scientists in Argentina unearth oldest tadpole fossil ever discovered
item.description: A search for dinosaur fossils unintentionally shifted into a discovery of the oldest tadpole ever discovered when a team exploring in Argentina came across the find.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Airlines pausing flights to Haiti after planes hit by gunfire
item.description: JetBlue, Spirit Airlines and American Airlines have paused flights to Haiti after two planes were struck by bullets amid escalating gang violence in the country.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9
